---
title: Documentation
description: Learn how to create and customize documentation sites using Nextra
icon: BookOpen
---

AeloKit uses [Nextra](https://nextra.site/) as the documentation system, providing a beautiful, responsive documentation interface.

## Core Features

* **MDX Support**: Use React components in Markdown
* **Full-text Search**: Built-in search functionality
* **Syntax Highlighting**: Automatic code block highlighting
* **Table of Contents**: Auto-generated page navigation
* **Dark Mode**: Built-in dark mode support
* **Internationalization**: Multi-language documentation support

## Documentation Structure

Documentation content is located in the `apps/web/content/docs` directory:

<Files>
  <Folder name="apps/web/content/docs" defaultOpen>
    <Folder name="config" defaultOpen>
      <File name="website.mdx" />
      <File name="navbar.mdx" />
      <File name="footer.mdx" />
    </Folder>
    <File name="index.mdx" />
    <File name="env.mdx" />
    <File name="database.mdx" />
    <File name="auth.mdx" />
  </Folder>
</Files>

### Document Metadata

Each document file starts with frontmatter:

```mdx
---
title: Document Title
description: Document Description
icon: IconName
---
```

### Documentation Configuration

Documentation configuration is defined in `apps/web/src/app/[locale]/docs/layout.tsx`:

```tsx
const docsOptions: DocsLayoutProps = {
  nav: {
    title: 'AeloKit',
    type: 'docs',
  },
  sidebar,
  toc: {
    backToTop: true,
  },
};
```

## Creating New Documents

1. Create a new `.mdx` file in the `apps/web/content/docs` directory
2. Add frontmatter metadata
3. Write document content
4. Add document entry in `meta.json`

### Example Document

```mdx
---
title: New Feature
description: Description of the new feature
icon: Star
---

## Overview

This is documentation for a new feature.

## Usage

Usage instructions go here.
```

### Adding to Navigation

Add the new document in `meta.json`:

```json
{
  "title": "New Feature",
  "href": "/new-feature"
}
```

## Internationalization

Documentation supports multiple languages:

* `docs.mdx` - English documentation
* `docs.zh.mdx` - Chinese documentation

### Translating Documents

1. Copy existing document file
2. Add language suffix (e.g., `.zh.mdx`)
3. Translate content
4. Keep the same file path

## Custom Components

Documents can use custom React components:

```mdx
import { Callout } from 'nextra/components';

<Callout type="info">
  This is an informational message.
</Callout>
```

### Available Components

* `Callout` - Callout box
* `Cards` - Card grid
* `Card` - Single card
* `Steps` - Step guide
* `Step` - Single step
* `Tabs` - Tabs
* `Tab` - Single tab

## Best Practices

1. **Keep it concise**: Documentation should be clear and concise
2. **Use examples**: Provide code examples and screenshots
3. **Organize structure**: Use heading hierarchy
4. **Update timely**: Keep documentation in sync with code
5. **Link related**: Link to related documentation

## References

* [Nextra Documentation](https://nextra.site/docs)
* [MDX Documentation](https://mdxjs.com/docs/)
